compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
# S6 â€” Pluggable custom verifier

## Why
No fixed set of verifiers covers every organization's identity system (bespoke internal token
services, non-standard introspection like GitHub's, mTLS-header trust). A pluggable seam keeps the
capability open-ended â€” an operator drops in their own verifier without patching rebar â€” which is
core to the "generic, wide variety of environments" goal.

## What
Add a **custom-verifier** mode to `src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py`, activated by including `custom` in the `mcp.auth_strategies` list (the strategy-selection
config key **introduced by S2**, this story's dependency â€” not a pre-existing seam), with one flat config key it registers: `auth_custom_import` (a `module:factory`
import string resolving to a factory that returns an object implementing the official SDK
`mcp.server.auth.provider.TokenVerifier` protocol). The factory's returned verifier is placed in the
composite and treated like any built-in â€” crucially, **the composite's audience re-check still
applies**: a custom verifier that returns an `AccessToken` with `resource=None` or a wrong `resource`
is rejected by the composite (S2), so a buggy/hostile custom verifier cannot bypass RFC 8707. Loading
validates the resolved object satisfies the protocol and fails closed otherwise. Adds the `auth_custom_import`
field to `McpConfig`/`_SECTIONS['mcp']` (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`); its derived
`REBAR_MCP_AUTH_CUSTOM_IMPORT` env-var name goes in `MCP_ENV_VARS` (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py`) +
`server.json`; `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes (docs regenerated by S7).

## Approach & rationale
Expose the SAME `TokenVerifier` seam the built-ins use, loaded via a config-named import path (the
established Python import-string convention, e.g. how uvicorn/Starlette resolve app factories). This
keeps custom verifiers ecosystem-compatible and adds no new abstraction. The composite audience
re-check (S2) is what makes an untrusted-in-code-quality custom verifier still safe on the audience
axis.

## Scope
The custom-verifier loader + protocol validation only, on the S2 seam. Subset of the epic. Depends on
S2. Independent of S3/S4/S5.

## Security
Boundary-crossing AND it loads operator-configured code (arbitrary import executes at load):
- The import path is an **operator-supplied trusted config value** and is treated as a documented
  trust boundary. It is read ONLY from resolved config (`auth_custom_import`), **never** from a
  request or any untrusted source â€” enforced structurally (the value is consumed only at startup from
  the config object), not merely documented.
- Import resolution uses `importlib.import_module` on the configured dotted path and does **not**
  insert the current working directory (`""`) into `sys.path`, so a module resolvable ONLY from the
  cwd is not loaded (avoids cwd shadowing/hijack). Resolution/validation failure, or a resolved
  object lacking `verify_token`, is a **fail-closed startup error** (never run with partial/no auth).
- The composite's mandatory audience re-check (S2) still applies to whatever the custom verifier
  returns â€” it cannot mint an accepted token for a wrong/absent audience.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `auth_custom_import` config field exists in `McpConfig`/`_SECTIONS['mcp']`
      (`_config_schema.py`); its derived `REBAR_MCP_AUTH_CUSTOM_IMPORT` env-var name is in
      `MCP_ENV_VARS` (`mcp_server.py`) + `server.json`; and `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes
      (observable oracle: manifest drift test green; both files contain the new env-var name). Docs
      regenerated by S7.
- [ ] Unit test (tier: unit) asserts a valid `module:factory` resolves (via `importlib`) to a working
      verifier whose accepted token (with `resource=auth_resource_server_url`) is honored by the
      composite (observable oracle: the composite accepts the token).
- [ ] Unit test asserts cwd is NOT consulted: a `module:factory` whose module exists ONLY in the
      current working directory (not installed / not on the configured import path) FAILS to load
      (observable oracle: loading raises a fail-closed error and the cwd module is not imported),
      proving the loader does not add cwd to `sys.path`.
- [ ] Unit test asserts fail-closed startup for: an unresolvable import path, and a resolved object
      that does not implement `verify_token` (observable oracle: startup raises a clear message) â€”
      two negative controls.
- [ ] Unit test asserts a custom verifier returning an `AccessToken` with `resource=None` or a wrong
      `resource` is **rejected by the composite** (the custom seam cannot bypass the audience check).
- [ ] Component test asserts a token accepted by a configured custom verifier (returning
      `resource=auth_resource_server_url`), invoked through the `CompositeTokenVerifier`'s verify path,
      is accepted; end-to-end HTTP behavior is exercised once for all verifiers by S2's transport
      harness, not re-tested here.
- [ ] `docs/mcp-auth.md` (S7) documents that `auth_custom_import` is a trusted operator config value
      that executes code at load.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

## Compatibility
Additive, opt-in via the `custom` strategy. No change to other verifiers or stdio.

file_impact.reason: add custom-verifier import loader + protocol validation
add derived env-var name to MCP_ENV_VARS

compiled_state.title: S6 â€” Pluggable custom verifier
